executive_summary: OmniQuantAI sees a HOLD recommendation for NVDA over the next 3-6 months, with a conditional path to add exposure only if portfolio sizing, earnings revisions, and macro triggers remain supportive.

key_evidence.finding: NVDA remains a high-quality AI infrastructure leader, but the demo evidence shows valuation and concentration risk are material.
Earnings momentum and AI demand remain supportive, while expectations are already elevated.
Higher-for-longer rates or liquidity stress would pressure long-duration growth equities.

evidence_cards.explanation: Momentum remains constructive, but valuation leaves less room for error.
AI infrastructure demand and data center revisions remain the key upside driver.
Rate shocks can compress long-duration growth multiples even if fundamentals hold.
A 4% NVDA weight can be increased only with staged sizing and drawdown controls.

investment_committee_memo.title: Investment Committee Memo
investment_committee_memo.investment_question: Should our fund increase exposure to Nvidia over the next 3-6 months?
investment_committee_memo.executive_summary: Maintain current exposure while monitoring earnings revisions, valuation discipline, macro rate sensitivity, and portfolio concentration. Consider staged increases only if the fund remains underweight and downside controls are explicit.
investment_committee_memo.bull_case: AI accelerator demand compounds, hyperscaler capex remains resilient, and NVDA earnings revisions outpace valuation risk.
investment_committee_memo.base_case: Fundamentals remain strong, but valuation limits near-term reward-to-risk. Keep exposure and reassess after earnings and macro confirmation.
investment_committee_memo.bear_case: Multiple compression, export controls, capex digestion, or a rate shock trigger a material drawdown.
investment_committee_memo.portfolio_considerations: The demo portfolio has NVDA at 4%. A staged increase is only appropriate if mandate limits allow more single-name AI exposure and drawdown triggers are pre-agreed.
what_would_change_view: Forward earnings revisions accelerate without multiple expansion
Portfolio NVDA weight moves above mandate cap
Two consecutive data center growth disappointments
investment_committee_memo.disclaimer: Not financial advice. Research support only. Human approval is required before allocation decisions.

bull_case: AI accelerator demand continues to exceed supply, hyperscaler capex remains resilient, and earnings revisions rise faster than valuation risk.
base_case: Maintain exposure while waiting for better reward-to-risk. Incremental buys require disciplined position sizing and confirmation from earnings revisions.
bear_case: Multiple compression, supply-chain bottlenecks, export restrictions, or a capex digestion cycle could make increased exposure unattractive.
risk_factors: High index and portfolio concentration in mega-cap AI names
Valuation sensitivity to rates and growth expectations
Potential 25-40% drawdown scenario if earnings revisions stall
what_would_change_view: Upgrade toward BUY if the fund is underweight NVDA and forward earnings revisions accelerate without valuation expansion.
Downgrade toward REDUCE if portfolio concentration breaches mandate limits or AI capex guidance weakens.
